steps.text: Hiding tools removes them from AI agent access without deleting your configuration. It's like putting a tool in maintenance mode - still configured, just not available to agents.
See the eye icon? That's the hide/show toggle. When the eye is open (visible), agents can call this tool. Click it to hide the tool from agents.
When you hide a tool, the card grays out and shows a 'Hidden' badge. The tool is still configured - your masking rules, mappers, everything stays intact.
What happens when an agent tries to call a hidden tool? They get a clean 'METHOD_NOT_FOUND' error, as if the tool doesn't exist. No access, no data leak!
When should you hide tools? Dangerous operations (delete_database, drop_table), deprecated tools, or tools you're testing. Better safe than sorry!
To unhide, just click the eye icon again. The tool becomes immediately available to agents. Toggle as needed - it's instant!
